\",\"endLine\":37,\"startLine\":33}],\"file\":\"pkg/login/social/social.go\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec/-/blob/pkg/login/social/social.go\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"48: \\n49: **Team Sync:** Able to sync teams from a predefined group/team in a your IdP\\n50: \\n51: **Allowed Groups:** Only allow members of certain groups to login\\n52: \\n53: **Active Sync:** Add users to teams and update their profile without requiring them to log in\\n54: \\n55: **Skip OrgRole Sync:** Able to modify org role for users and not sync it back to the IdP\\n56: \\n57: **Auto Login:** Automatically redirects to provider login page if user is not logged in \\\\* for OAuth; Only works if it's the only configured provider\\n58: \\n59: \",\"endLine\":59,\"startLine\":48},{\"content\":\"17: \\n18: Grafana provides many ways to authenticate users. Some authentication integrations also enable syncing user permissions and org memberships.\\n19: \\n20: The following table shows all supported authentication methods and the features available for them. [Team sync](../configure-team-sync/) and [active sync](enhanced-ldap/#active-ldap-synchronization) are only available in Grafana Enterprise.\\n21: \\n22: | Authentication method               | Multi Org Mapping | Enforce Sync | Role Mapping | Grafana Admin Mapping | Team Sync | Allowed groups | Active Sync | Skip OrgRole mapping | Auto Login | Single Logout | SCIM support |\\n23: | :---------------------------------- | :---------------- | :----------- | :----------- | :-------------------- | :-------- | :------------- | :---------- | :------------------- | :--------- | :------------ | :----------- |\\n24: \",\"endLine\":24,\"startLine\":17},{\"content\":\"73: - Check if the identity provider supports account federation. In such cases, you can configure it once and let your identity provider federate the accounts from different providers.\\n74: - If SAML is supported by the identity provider, you can configure one [Generic OAuth](generic-oauth/) and one [SAML](saml/) (Enterprise only).\\n75: \\n76: \",\"endLine\":76,\"startLine\":73}],\"file\":\"docs/sources/setup-grafana/configure-security/configure-authentication/_index.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec/-/blob/docs/sources/setup-grafana/configure-security/configure-authentication/_index.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"175: \\n176: ## User synchronization between Grafana and identity providers\\n177: \\n178: When connecting Grafana to an identity provider, it's important to think beyond just the initial authentication setup. You should also think about the maintenance of user bases and roles. Using Grafana's team and role synchronization features ensures that  ... [line truncated]\\n179: \\n180: ### Team sync\\n181: \\n182: Team sync is a feature that allows you to synchronize teams or groups from your authentication provider with teams in Grafana. This means that users of specific teams or groups in LDAP, OAuth, or SAML will be automatically added or removed as members of co ... [line truncated]\\n183: \\n184: \",\"endLine\":184,\"startLine\":175},{\"content\":\"202: {{< admonition type=\\\"note\\\" >}}\\n203: Organization sync is currently only supported for SAML and LDAP.\\n204: {{< /admonition >}}\\n205: \",\"endLine\":205,\"startLine\":202},{\"content\":\"188: {{< admonition type=\\\"note\\\" >}}\\n189: Team synchronization occurs only when a user logs in. However, if you are using LDAP, it is possible to enable active background synchronization. This allows for the continuous synchronization of teams.\\n190: {{< /admonition >}}\\n191: \",\"endLine\":191,\"startLine\":188}],\"file\":\"docs/sources/setup-grafana/configure-security/planning-iam-strategy/index.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec/-/blob/docs/sources/setup-grafana/configure-security/planning-iam-strategy/index.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"246: \\n247: {{< figure src=\\\"/static/img/docs/enterprise/oauth-synced-user-9-1.png\\\" max-width=\\\"750px\\\" caption=\\\"Non-interactive view of a user synced via OAuth\\\" >}}\\n248: \\n249: #### Support wildcards for LDAP groups in team sync\\n250: \\n251: Team sync lets you set up synchronization between your authentication provider's teams and Grafana teams.\\n252: To leverage your existing Active Directory in an efficient way without having to create multiple teams, you can now use wildcards when configuring LDAP groups, so that multiple Active Directory groups can be added at once to a single team.\\n253: \\n254: {{< figure src=\\\"/static/img/docs/enterprise/ldap-wildcard-teamsync-9-1.png\\\" max-width=\\\"750px\\\" caption=\\\"Using wildcard mapping for team sync\\\" >}}\\n255: \\n256: #### Redirect binding support for AzureAD SAML Single Logout\\n257: \\n258: SAML Single Logout allows users to log out from all applications associated with the current IdP (Identity Provider) session established via SAML Single Sign-On (SSO).\\n259: ... [chunk truncated]\",\"endLine\":260,\"startLine\":246},{\"content\":\"239: \\n240: #### See (and don't edit) users synced from SAML, LDAP, and OAuth identity providers\\n241: \\n242: When you synchronize users from a SAML, LDAP, or OAuth provider, some user settings, such as name and email address, are synchronized from your identity provider.\\n243: Previously, you could edit those settings in the Grafana UI, but they would revert back.\\n244: To make user management clearer, you can now see which settings are synchronized from your identity provider, but you cannot edit those settings.\\n245: To learn more about authentication, see the [documentation](../../setup-grafana/configure-security/configure-authentication/).\\n246: \",\"endLine\":246,\"startLine\":239},{\"content\":\"56: \\n57: #### Organization role mapping for GitHub OAuth2 authentication\\n58: \\n59: You can now use GitHub OAuth2 to map users or teams to specific [Grafana organization roles](../../administration/roles-and-permissions/#organization-roles) by using `role_attribute_path` configuration option.\\n60: Grafana will use [JMESPath](https://jmespath.org/examples.html) for path lookup and role mapping.\\n61: \",\"endLine\":61,\"startLine\":56}],\"file\":\"docs/sources/whatsnew/whats-new-in-v9-1.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec/-/blob/docs/sources/whatsnew/whats-new-in-v9-1.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"613: \\n614: \\t// FlagGroupAttributeSync\\n615: \\t// Enable the groupsync extension for managing Group Attribute Sync feature\\n616: \\tFlagGroupAttributeSync = \\\"groupAttributeSync\\\"\\n617: \\n618: \",\"endLine\":618,\"startLine\":613},{\"content\":\"769: \\n770: \\t// FlagImprovedExternalSessionHandlingSAML\\n771: \\t// Enables improved support for SAML external sessions. Ensure the NameID format is correctly configured in Grafana for SAML Single Logout to function properly.\\n772: \\tFlagImprovedExternalSessionHandlingSAML = \\\"improvedExternalSessionHandlingSAML\\\"\\n773: \\n774: \\t// FlagTeamHttpHeadersTempo\\n775: \\t// Enables LBAC for datasources for Tempo to apply LBAC filtering of traces to the client requests for users in teams\\n776: \\tFlagTeamHttpHeadersTempo = \\\"teamHttpHeadersTempo\\\"\\n777: \\n778: \",\"endLine\":778,\"startLine\":769},{\"content\":\"1048: \\n1049: \\t// FlagTeamFolders\\n1050: \\t// Enables team folders functionality\\n1051: \\tFlagTeamFolders = \\\"teamFolders\\\"\\n1052: \\n1053: \",\"endLine\":1053,\"startLine\":1048}],\"file\":\"pkg/services/featuremgmt/toggles_gen.go\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec/-/blob/pkg/services/featuremgmt/toggles_gen.go\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"213: \\n214: If you use a SAML identity provider to manage your users but prefer to assign roles and permissions in the Grafana UI or via API, you can now enable a configuration option to skip user organization and roles synchronization with your SAML provider.\\n215: \\n216: Use the `skip_org_role_sync` configuration option when configuring SAML to prevent synchronization with SAML roles and make user roles editable from within Grafana.\\n217: \\n218: For more information, see the [SAML configuration documentation](/docs/grafana/latest/setup-grafana/configure-security/configure-authentication/saml/).\\n219: \\n220: ## Assign Server Admin permissions from Oauth\\n221: \\n222: You can now map OAuth groups and roles to Server Admin for the GitLab, GitHub, AzureAD, Okta, and Generic OAuth integrations.\\n223: To enable this functionality, set the `allow_assign_grafana_admin` configuration option to `true` in the desired OAuth integration section.\\n224: ... [chunk truncated]\",\"endLine\":226,\"startLine\":213},{\"content\":\"170: \\n171: You can now leave a team empty without any users, or have only Members in a team.\\n172: This helps you sync teams and users from a single sign-on provider like Active Directory or Okta, or if you use teams as collections of permissions.\\n173: Previously, teams required at least one Admin user to be in a team.\\n174: \\n175: {{< figure src=\\\"/static/img/docs/manage-users/member-only-team-9-2.png\\\" max-width=\\\"750px\\\" caption=\\\"Creating a team without an Admin user\\\" >}}\\n176: \\n177: \",\"endLine\":177,\"startLine\":170},{\"content\":\"206: \\n207: You can now use `*` as the Grafana organization in the mapping to add all users from a given SAML Organization to all existing Grafana organizations.\\n208: For more information, see [\\\"Configure SAML authentication\\\"](/docs/grafana/next/setup-grafana/configure-security/configure-authentication/saml/#configure-organization-mapping) in the documentation.\\n209: \\n210: ### Skip organization role sync\\n211: \\n212: \",\"endLine\":212,\"startLine\":206}],\"file\":\"docs/sources/whatsnew/whats-new-in-v9-2.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec/-/blob/docs/sources/whatsnew/whats-new-in-v9-2.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"86: \\n87: \\t// LDAPUsersSyncExecutionTime is a metric summary for LDAP users sync execution duration\\n88: \\tLDAPUsersSyncExecutionTime prometheus.Summary\\n89: \\n90: \",\"endLine\":90,\"startLine\":86},{\"content\":\"787: \\t\\tMDBDataSourceQueryByID,\\n788: \\t\\tLDAPUsersSyncExecutionTime,\\n789: \\t\\tMRenderingRequestTotal,\\n790: \",\"endLine\":790,\"startLine\":787},{\"content\":\"404: \\n405: \\tLDAPUsersSyncExecutionTime = prometheus.NewSummary(prometheus.SummaryOpts{\\n406: \\t\\tName:       \\\"ldap_users_sync_execution_time\\\",\\n407: \\t\\tHelp:       \\\"summary for LDAP users sync execution duration\\\",\\n408: \\t\\tObjectives: objectiveMap,\\n409: \",\"endLine\":409,\"startLine\":404}],\"file\":\"pkg/infra/metrics/metrics.go\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec/-/blob/pkg/infra/metrics/metrics.go\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"671:   /**\\n672:   * Enable the groupsync extension for managing Group Attribute Sync feature\\n673:   */\\n674:   groupAttributeSync?: boolean;\\n675:   /**\\n676: \",\"endLine\":676,\"startLine\":671},{\"content\":\"851:   */\\n852:   improvedExternalSessionHandlingSAML?: boolean;\\n853:   /**\\n854:   * Enables LBAC for datasources for Tempo to apply LBAC filtering of traces to the client requests for users in teams\\n855:   */\\n856:   teamHttpHeadersTempo?: boolean;\\n857:   /**\\n858: \",\"endLine\":858,\"startLine\":851},{\"content\":\"1166:   */\\n1167:   teamFolders?: boolean;\\n1168:   /**\\n1169: \",\"endLine\":1169,\"startLine\":1166}],\"file\":\"packages/grafana-data/src/types/featureToggles.gen.ts\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec/-/blob/packages/grafana-data/src/types/featureToggles.gen.ts\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"117: {{< admonition type=\\\"warning\\\" >}}\\n118: **Team Sync Compatibility**:\\n119: \\n120: - SCIM group sync (`group_sync_enabled = true`) and Team Sync cannot be enabled simultaneously\\n121: - You can use SCIM user sync (`user_sync_enabled = true`) alongside Team Sync\\n122: - For more details about migration and compatibility, see [SCIM vs Team Sync](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/<GRAFANA_VERSION>/setup-grafana/configure-security/configure-scim-provisioning/manage-users-teams/#scim-vs-team-sync)\\n123:   {{< /admonition >}}\\n124: \",\"endLine\":124,\"startLine\":117},{\"content\":\"93: | ------------------------------ | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------- |\\n94: | `Enable Group Sync`            | No       | Enable SCIM group provisioning. When enabled, Grafana will create, update, and delete teams based on SCIM requests from your identity provider. Cannot be enabled if Team Sync is enabled. | `false` |\\n95: | `Reject Non-Provisioned Users` | No       | When enabled, prevents non-SCIM provisioned users from signing in. Cloud Portal users can always sign in regardless of this setting.                                                       | `false` |\\n96: | `Enable User Sync`             | Yes      | Enable SCIM user provisioning. When enabled, Grafana will create, update, and deactivate users based on SCIM requests from your identity provider.                                         | `false` |\\n97: \\n98: \",\"endLine\":98,\"startLine\":93},{\"content\":\"153: | ------------------------------ | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------- |\\n154: | `user_sync_enabled`            | Yes      | Enable SCIM user provisioning. When enabled, Grafana will create, update, and deactivate users based on SCIM requests from your identity provider.                                         | `false` |\\n155: | `group_sync_enabled`           | No       | Enable SCIM group provisioning. When enabled, Grafana will create, update, and delete teams based on SCIM requests from your identity provider. Cannot be enabled if Team Sync is enabled. | `false` |\\n156: | `reject_non_provisioned_users` | No       | When enabled, prevents non-SCIM provisioned users from signing in. Cloud Portal users can always sign in regardless of this setting.                                                       | `false` |\\n157: \",\"endLine\":157,\"startLine\":153}],\"file\":\"docs/sources/setup-grafana/configure-security/configure-scim-provisioning/_index.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec/-/blob/docs/sources/setup-grafana/configure-security/configure-scim-provisioning/_index.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"43: var (\\n44: \\tldapReaderRole = RoleDTO{\\n45: \\t\\tName:        \\\"fixed:ldap:reader\\\",\\n46: \\t\\tDisplayName: \\\"Reader\\\",\\n47: \\t\\tDescription: \\\"Read LDAP configuration and status.\\\",\\n48: \\t\\tGroup:       \\\"LDAP\\\",\\n49: \\t\\tPermissions: []Permission{\\n50: \",\"endLine\":50,\"startLine\":43},{\"content\":\"58: \\n59: \\tldapWriterRole = RoleDTO{\\n60: \\t\\tName:        \\\"fixed:ldap:writer\\\",\\n61: \\t\\tDisplayName: \\\"Writer\\\",\\n62: \\t\\tDescription: \\\"Read and update LDAP configuration and read LDAP status.\\\",\\n63: \\t\\tGroup:       \\\"LDAP\\\",\\n64: \\t\\tPermissions: ConcatPermissions(ldapReaderRole.Permissions, []Permission{\\n65: \\t\\t\\t{\\n66: \\t\\t\\t\\tAction: ActionLDAPUsersSync,\\n67: \\t\\t\\t},\\n68: \",\"endLine\":68,\"startLine\":58},{\"content\":\"269: \\t\\t\\t\\tAction: ActionSettingsRead,\\n270: \\t\\t\\t\\tScope:  ScopeSettingsOAuth(\\\"ldap\\\"),\\n271: \\t\\t\\t},\\n272: \",\"endLine\":272,\"startLine\":269}],\"file\":\"pkg/services/accesscontrol/roles.go\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec/-/blob/pkg/services/accesscontrol/roles.go\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"424: \\n425: func (hs *HTTPServer) samlSkipOrgRoleSyncEnabled() bool {\\n426: \\tconfig, ok := hs.authnService.GetClientConfig(authn.ClientSAML)\\n427: \\tif !ok {\\n428: \",\"endLine\":428,\"startLine\":424},{\"content\":\"474: // https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/4181acec72f76df7ad02badce13769bae4a1f840/pkg/services/login/authinfoservice/database/database.go#L61\\n475: // this means that if the user has multiple auth providers and one of them is set to sync org roles\\n476: // then isExternallySynced will be true for this one provider and false for the others\\n477: func (hs *HTTPServer) isExternallySynced(cfg *setting.Cfg, authModule string) bool {\\n478: \\t// provider enabled in config\\n479: \",\"endLine\":479,\"startLine\":474},{\"content\":\"501: \\n502: // isGrafanaAdminExternallySynced returns true if Grafana server admin role is being managed by an external auth provider, and false otherwise.\\n503: // Grafana admin role sync is available for JWT, OAuth providers and LDAP.\\n504: // For JWT and OAuth providers there is an additional config option `allow_assign_grafana_admin` that has to be enabled for Grafana Admin role to be synced.\\n505: func (hs *HTTPServer) isGrafanaAdminExternallySynced(cfg *setting.Cfg, authModule string) bool {\\n506: \\tif !hs.isExternallySynced(cfg, authModule) {\\n507: \\t\\treturn false\\n508: \",\"endLine\":508,\"startLine\":501}],\"file\":\"pkg/api/login.go\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec/-/blob/pkg/api/login.go\"}]}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "blocks": [
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "31: \n32: - [Auth Proxy](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/<GRAFANA_VERSION>/setup-grafana/configure-security/configure-authentication/auth-proxy/#team-sync)\n33: - [Azure AD](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/<GRAFANA_VERSION>/setup-grafana/configure-security/configure-authentication/azuread/#team-sync)\n34: - [Generic OAuth integration](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/<GRAFANA_VERSION>/setup-grafana/configure-security/configure-authentication/generic-oauth/#configure-team-synchronization)\n35: - [GitHub OAuth](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/<GRAFANA_VERSION>/setup-grafana/configure-security/configure-authentication/github/#configure-team-synchronization)\n36: - [GitLab OAuth](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/<GRAFANA_VERSION>/setup-grafana/configure-security/configure-authentication/gitlab/#configure-team-synchronization)\n37: - [Google OAuth](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/<GRAFANA_VERSION>/setup-grafana/configure-security/configure-authentication/google/#configure-team-synchronization)\n38: ... [chunk truncated]",
            "endLine": 67,
            "startLine": 31
          },
          {
            "content": "14: \n15: # Configure Team Sync\n16: \n17: Team sync lets you set up synchronization between your auth providers teams and teams in Grafana. This enables LDAP, OAuth, or SAML users who are members of certain teams or groups to automatically be added or removed as members of certain teams in Grafana ... [line truncated]\n18: \n19: ",
            "endLine": 19,
            "startLine": 14
          },
          {
            "content": "22: \n23: Grafana keeps track of all synchronized users in teams, and you can see which users have been synchronized in the team members list, see `LDAP` label in screenshot.\n24: This mechanism allows Grafana to remove an existing synchronized user from a team when its group membership changes. This mechanism also enables you to manually add a user as member of a team, and it will not be removed when the user signs in. This gives y ... [line truncated]\n25: \n26: > Currently the synchronization only happens when a user logs in, unless LDAP is used with the active background synchronization.\n27: \n28: ",
            "endLine": 28,
            "startLine": 22
          }
        ],
        "file": "docs/sources/setup-grafana/configure-security/configure-team-sync.md",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec/-/blob/docs/sources/setup-grafana/configure-security/configure-team-sync.md"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "369: \n370: \t// LDAP actions\n371: \tActionLDAPUsersRead    = \"ldap.user:read\"\n372: \tActionLDAPUsersSync    = \"ldap.user:sync\"\n373: \tActionLDAPStatusRead   = \"ldap.status:read\"\n374: \tActionLDAPConfigReload = \"ldap.config:reload\"\n375: \n376: ",
            "endLine": 376,
            "startLine": 369
          },
          {
            "content": "414: \n415: \t// Alert scopes are divided into two groups. The internal (to Grafana) and the external ones.\n416: \t// For the Grafana ones, given we have ACID control we're able to provide better granularity by defining CRUD options.\n417: \t// For the external ones, we only have read and write permissions due to the lack of atomicity control of the external system.\n418: \n419: ",
            "endLine": 419,
            "startLine": 414
          },
          {
            "content": "397: \n398: \t// Team related actions\n399: \tActionTeamsCreate           = \"teams:create\"\n400: \tActionTeamsDelete           = \"teams:delete\"\n401: \tActionTeamsRead             = \"teams:read\"\n402: \tActionTeamsWrite            = \"teams:write\"\n403: \tActionTeamsPermissionsRead  = \"teams.permissions:read\"\n404: \tActionTeamsPermissionsWrite = \"teams.permissions:write\"\n405: \n406: \t// Team related scopes\n407: \tScopeTeamsAll = \"teams:*\"\n408: \n409: ",
            "endLine": 409,
            "startLine": 397
          }
        ],
        "file": "pkg/services/accesscontrol/models.go",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec/-/blob/pkg/services/accesscontrol/models.go"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "41: \n42: const headerTooltip = `Sync LDAP, OAuth or SAML groups with your Grafana teams.`;\n43: \n44: export class TeamGroupSync extends PureComponent<Props, State> {\n45:   constructor(props: Props) {\n46: ",
            "endLine": 46,
            "startLine": 41
          },
          {
            "content": "122:               <h3 className=\"page-sub-heading\">\n123:                 <Trans i18nKey=\"teams.team-group-sync.external-group-sync\">External group sync</Trans>\n124:               </h3>\n125: ",
            "endLine": 125,
            "startLine": 122
          },
          {
            "content": "143:                 <InlineField\n144:                   label={t('teams.team-group-sync.label-add-external-group', 'Add external group')}\n145:                   tooltip={t('teams.team-group-sync.tooltip-add-external-group', 'LDAP group example: {{example}}', {\n146:                     example: 'cn=users,ou=groups,dc=grafana,dc=org',\n147:                   })}\n148: ",
            "endLine": 148,
            "startLine": 143
          }
        ],
        "file": "public/app/features/teams/TeamGroupSync.tsx",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec/-/blob/public/app/features/teams/TeamGroupSync.tsx"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "37: \tName           string\n38: \tGroups         []string\n39: \tOrgRoles       map[int64]org.RoleType\n40: \tIsGrafanaAdmin *bool // This is a pointer to know if we should sync this or not (nil = ignore sync)\n41: \tIsDisabled     bool\n42: \tSkipTeamSync   bool\n43: }\n44: ",
            "endLine": 44,
            "startLine": 37
          },
          {
            "content": "54: \t}\n55: \treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"OAuthToken: %+v, AuthModule: %v, AuthId: %v, UserId: %v, Email: %v, Login: %v, Name: %v, Groups: %v, OrgRoles: %v, IsGrafanaAdmin: %v, IsDisabled: %v, SkipTeamSync: %v\",\n56: \t\te.OAuthToken, e.AuthModule, e.AuthId, e.UserId, e.Email, e.Login, e.Name, e.Groups, e.OrgRoles, isGrafanaAdmin, e.IsDisabled, e.SkipTeamSync)\n57: }\n58: ",
            "endLine": 58,
            "startLine": 54
          },
          {
            "content": "78: \tUserId      int64\n79: \tOAuthToken  *oauth2.Token\n80: \tExternalUID string\n81: }\n82: ",

          }
        ],
        "file": "pkg/services/login/model.go",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec",

        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec/-/blob/pkg/services/login/model.go"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "127: \n128: func (o *OAuthInfo) IsSkipOrgRoleSyncEnabled() bool {\n129: \treturn o.SkipOrgRoleSync\n130: }\n131: ",
            "endLine": 131,
            "startLine": 127
          },
          {
            "content": "53: \n54: \tGetOAuthInfo() *OAuthInfo\n55: \n56: \tAuthCodeURL(state string, opts ...oauth2.AuthCodeOption) string\n57: \tExchange(ctx context.Context, code string, authOptions ...oauth2.AuthCodeOption) (*oauth2.Token, error)\n58: \tClient(ctx context.Context, t *oauth2.Token) *http.Client\n59: \tTokenSource(ctx context.Context, t *oauth2.Token) oauth2.TokenSource\n60: \tSupportBundleContent(*bytes.Buffer) error\n61: ",
            "endLine": 61,
            "startLine": 53
          },
          {
            "content": "33: \tOktaProviderName       = \"okta\"\n34: \tSAMLProviderName       = \"saml\"\n35: \tLDAPProviderName       = \"ldap\"\n36: )\n37: ",
            "endLine": 37,
            "startLine": 33
          }
        ],
        "file": "pkg/login/social/social.go",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec/-/blob/pkg/login/social/social.go"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "48: \n49: **Team Sync:** Able to sync teams from a predefined group/team in a your IdP\n50: \n51: **Allowed Groups:** Only allow members of certain groups to login\n52: \n53: **Active Sync:** Add users to teams and update their profile without requiring them to log in\n54: \n55: **Skip OrgRole Sync:** Able to modify org role for users and not sync it back to the IdP\n56: \n57: **Auto Login:** Automatically redirects to provider login page if user is not logged in \\* for OAuth; Only works if it's the only configured provider\n58: \n59: ",
            "endLine": 59,
            "startLine": 48
          },
          {
            "content": "17: \n18: Grafana provides many ways to authenticate users. Some authentication integrations also enable syncing user permissions and org memberships.\n19: \n20: The following table shows all supported authentication methods and the features available for them. [Team sync](../configure-team-sync/) and [active sync](enhanced-ldap/#active-ldap-synchronization) are only available in Grafana Enterprise.\n21: \n22: | Authentication method               | Multi Org Mapping | Enforce Sync | Role Mapping | Grafana Admin Mapping | Team Sync | Allowed groups | Active Sync | Skip OrgRole mapping | Auto Login | Single Logout | SCIM support |\n23: | :---------------------------------- | :---------------- | :----------- | :----------- | :-------------------- | :-------- | :------------- | :---------- | :------------------- | :--------- | :------------ | :----------- |\n24: ",
            "endLine": 24,
            "startLine": 17
          },
          {
            "content": "73: - Check if the identity provider supports account federation. In such cases, you can configure it once and let your identity provider federate the accounts from different providers.\n74: - If SAML is supported by the identity provider, you can configure one [Generic OAuth](generic-oauth/) and one [SAML](saml/) (Enterprise only).\n75: \n76: ",
            "endLine": 76,
            "startLine": 73
          }
        ],
        "file": "docs/sources/setup-grafana/configure-security/configure-authentication/_index.md",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec/-/blob/docs/sources/setup-grafana/configure-security/configure-authentication/_index.md"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "175: \n176: ## User synchronization between Grafana and identity providers\n177: \n178: When connecting Grafana to an identity provider, it's important to think beyond just the initial authentication setup. You should also think about the maintenance of user bases and roles. Using Grafana's team and role synchronization features ensures that  ... [line truncated]\n179: \n180: ### Team sync\n181: \n182: Team sync is a feature that allows you to synchronize teams or groups from your authentication provider with teams in Grafana. This means that users of specific teams or groups in LDAP, OAuth, or SAML will be automatically added or removed as members of co ... [line truncated]\n183: \n184: ",
            "endLine": 184,
            "startLine": 175
          },
          {
            "content": "202: {{< admonition type=\"note\" >}}\n203: Organization sync is currently only supported for SAML and LDAP.\n204: {{< /admonition >}}\n205: ",
            "endLine": 205,
            "startLine": 202
          },
          {
            "content": "188: {{< admonition type=\"note\" >}}\n189: Team synchronization occurs only when a user logs in. However, if you are using LDAP, it is possible to enable active background synchronization. This allows for the continuous synchronization of teams.\n190: {{< /admonition >}}\n191: ",
            "endLine": 191,
            "startLine": 188
          }
        ],
        "file": "docs/sources/setup-grafana/configure-security/planning-iam-strategy/index.md",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec/-/blob/docs/sources/setup-grafana/configure-security/planning-iam-strategy/index.md"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "246: \n247: {{< figure src=\"/static/img/docs/enterprise/oauth-synced-user-9-1.png\" max-width=\"750px\" caption=\"Non-interactive view of a user synced via OAuth\" >}}\n248: \n249: #### Support wildcards for LDAP groups in team sync\n250: \n251: Team sync lets you set up synchronization between your authentication provider's teams and Grafana teams.\n252: To leverage your existing Active Directory in an efficient way without having to create multiple teams, you can now use wildcards when configuring LDAP groups, so that multiple Active Directory groups can be added at once to a single team.\n253: \n254: {{< figure src=\"/static/img/docs/enterprise/ldap-wildcard-teamsync-9-1.png\" max-width=\"750px\" caption=\"Using wildcard mapping for team sync\" >}}\n255: \n256: #### Redirect binding support for AzureAD SAML Single Logout\n257: \n258: SAML Single Logout allows users to log out from all applications associated with the current IdP (Identity Provider) session established via SAML Single Sign-On (SSO).\n259: ... [chunk truncated]",
            "endLine": 260,
            "startLine": 246
          },
          {
            "content": "239: \n240: #### See (and don't edit) users synced from SAML, LDAP, and OAuth identity providers\n241: \n242: When you synchronize users from a SAML, LDAP, or OAuth provider, some user settings, such as name and email address, are synchronized from your identity provider.\n243: Previously, you could edit those settings in the Grafana UI, but they would revert back.\n244: To make user management clearer, you can now see which settings are synchronized from your identity provider, but you cannot edit those settings.\n245: To learn more about authentication, see the [documentation](../../setup-grafana/configure-security/configure-authentication/).\n246: ",
            "endLine": 246,
            "startLine": 239
          },
          {
            "content": "56: \n57: #### Organization role mapping for GitHub OAuth2 authentication\n58: \n59: You can now use GitHub OAuth2 to map users or teams to specific [Grafana organization roles](../../administration/roles-and-permissions/#organization-roles) by using `role_attribute_path` configuration option.\n60: Grafana will use [JMESPath](https://jmespath.org/examples.html) for path lookup and role mapping.\n61: ",
            "endLine": 61,
            "startLine": 56
          }
        ],
        "file": "docs/sources/whatsnew/whats-new-in-v9-1.md",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec/-/blob/docs/sources/whatsnew/whats-new-in-v9-1.md"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "613: \n614: \t// FlagGroupAttributeSync\n615: \t// Enable the groupsync extension for managing Group Attribute Sync feature\n616: \tFlagGroupAttributeSync = \"groupAttributeSync\"\n617: \n618: ",
            "endLine": 618,
            "startLine": 613
          },
          {
            "content": "769: \n770: \t// FlagImprovedExternalSessionHandlingSAML\n771: \t// Enables improved support for SAML external sessions. Ensure the NameID format is correctly configured in Grafana for SAML Single Logout to function properly.\n772: \tFlagImprovedExternalSessionHandlingSAML = \"improvedExternalSessionHandlingSAML\"\n773: \n774: \t// FlagTeamHttpHeadersTempo\n775: \t// Enables LBAC for datasources for Tempo to apply LBAC filtering of traces to the client requests for users in teams\n776: \tFlagTeamHttpHeadersTempo = \"teamHttpHeadersTempo\"\n777: \n778: ",
            "endLine": 778,
            "startLine": 769
          },
          {
            "content": "1048: \n1049: \t// FlagTeamFolders\n1050: \t// Enables team folders functionality\n1051: \tFlagTeamFolders = \"teamFolders\"\n1052: \n1053: ",
            "endLine": 1053,
            "startLine": 1048
          }
        ],
        "file": "pkg/services/featuremgmt/toggles_gen.go",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec/-/blob/pkg/services/featuremgmt/toggles_gen.go"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "213: \n214: If you use a SAML identity provider to manage your users but prefer to assign roles and permissions in the Grafana UI or via API, you can now enable a configuration option to skip user organization and roles synchronization with your SAML provider.\n215: \n216: Use the `skip_org_role_sync` configuration option when configuring SAML to prevent synchronization with SAML roles and make user roles editable from within Grafana.\n217: \n218: For more information, see the [SAML configuration documentation](/docs/grafana/latest/setup-grafana/configure-security/configure-authentication/saml/).\n219: \n220: ## Assign Server Admin permissions from Oauth\n221: \n222: You can now map OAuth groups and roles to Server Admin for the GitLab, GitHub, AzureAD, Okta, and Generic OAuth integrations.\n223: To enable this functionality, set the `allow_assign_grafana_admin` configuration option to `true` in the desired OAuth integration section.\n224: ... [chunk truncated]",
            "endLine": 226,
            "startLine": 213
          },
          {
            "content": "170: \n171: You can now leave a team empty without any users, or have only Members in a team.\n172: This helps you sync teams and users from a single sign-on provider like Active Directory or Okta, or if you use teams as collections of permissions.\n173: Previously, teams required at least one Admin user to be in a team.\n174: \n175: {{< figure src=\"/static/img/docs/manage-users/member-only-team-9-2.png\" max-width=\"750px\" caption=\"Creating a team without an Admin user\" >}}\n176: \n177: ",
            "endLine": 177,
            "startLine": 170
          },
          {
            "content": "206: \n207: You can now use `*` as the Grafana organization in the mapping to add all users from a given SAML Organization to all existing Grafana organizations.\n208: For more information, see [\"Configure SAML authentication\"](/docs/grafana/next/setup-grafana/configure-security/configure-authentication/saml/#configure-organization-mapping) in the documentation.\n209: \n210: ### Skip organization role sync\n211: \n212: ",
            "endLine": 212,
            "startLine": 206
          }
        ],

      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "117: {{< admonition type=\"warning\" >}}\n118: **Team Sync Compatibility**:\n119: \n120: - SCIM group sync (`group_sync_enabled = true`) and Team Sync cannot be enabled simultaneously\n121: - You can use SCIM user sync (`user_sync_enabled = true`) alongside Team Sync\n122: - For more details about migration and compatibility, see [SCIM vs Team Sync](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/<GRAFANA_VERSION>/setup-grafana/configure-security/configure-scim-provisioning/manage-users-teams/#scim-vs-team-sync)\n123:   {{< /admonition >}}\n124: ",
            "endLine": 124,
            "startLine": 117
          },
          {
            "content": "93: | ------------------------------ | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------- |\n94: | `Enable Group Sync`            | No       | Enable SCIM group provisioning. When enabled, Grafana will create, update, and delete teams based on SCIM requests from your identity provider. Cannot be enabled if Team Sync is enabled. | `false` |\n95: | `Reject Non-Provisioned Users` | No       | When enabled, prevents non-SCIM provisioned users from signing in. Cloud Portal users can always sign in regardless of this setting.                                                       | `false` |\n96: | `Enable User Sync`             | Yes      | Enable SCIM user provisioning. When enabled, Grafana will create, update, and deactivate users based on SCIM requests from your identity provider.                                         | `false` |\n97: \n98: ",
            "endLine": 98,
            "startLine": 93
          },
          {
            "content": "153: | ------------------------------ | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------- |\n154: | `user_sync_enabled`            | Yes      | Enable SCIM user provisioning. When enabled, Grafana will create, update, and deactivate users based on SCIM requests from your identity provider.                                         | `false` |\n155: | `group_sync_enabled`           | No       | Enable SCIM group provisioning. When enabled, Grafana will create, update, and delete teams based on SCIM requests from your identity provider. Cannot be enabled if Team Sync is enabled. | `false` |\n156: | `reject_non_provisioned_users` | No       | When enabled, prevents non-SCIM provisioned users from signing in. Cloud Portal users can always sign in regardless of this setting.                                                       | `false` |\n157: ",
            "endLine": 157,
            "startLine": 153
          }
        ],
        "file": "docs/sources/setup-grafana/configure-security/configure-scim-provisioning/_index.md",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec",

24. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_keyword_search @ -
Input
{
  "query": "repo:^github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec$ file:.*team.*.go groups external"

Output
{
  "content": "{\"blocks\":[{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"53: \\n54: \\t\\tif tapi.license.FeatureEnabled(\\\"teamgroupsync\\\") && member.External {\\n55: \\t\\t\\tauthProvider := login.GetAuthProviderLabel(member.AuthModule)\\n56: \",\"endLine\":56,\"startLine\":53},{\"content\":\"301: \\n302: \\tisGroupSyncEnabled := tapi.cfg.Raw.Section(\\\"auth.scim\\\").Key(\\\"group_sync_enabled\\\").MustBool(false)\\n303: \\tif isGroupSyncEnabled && existingTeam.IsProvisioned {\\n304: \\t\\treturn response.Error(http.StatusBadRequest, \\\"Team memberships cannot be updated for provisioned teams\\\", err)\\n305: \",\"endLine\":305,\"startLine\":301}],\"file\":\"pkg/services/team/teamapi/team_members.go\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec/-/blob/pkg/services/team/teamapi/team_members.go\"}]}",
  "structuredContent": {

    {
      "text": "Based on my research, I've found all the key files related to team group synchronization. Let me compile the findings:\n\n## Summary of Team Group Synchronization Files in Grafana\n\nBased on my comprehensive search of the grafana/grafana codebase, here are the exact file paths that implement the team group synchronization feature:\n\n### Frontend Components:\n\n1. **UI Component for Team Group Sync**:\n   - `/workspace/public/app/features/teams/TeamGroupSync.tsx` - React component that displays the external group sync UI\n\n2. **Navigation Model**:\n   - `/workspace/public/app/features/teams/state/navModel.ts` - Defines the External group sync tab in team navigation\n\n3. **API Client Hooks**:\n   - `/workspace/packages/grafana-api-clients/src/clients/rtkq/legacy/endpoints.gen.ts` - Generated API client endpoints for team groups\n\n### Backend API Implementation:\n\n4. **Team API Routes**:\n   - `/workspace/pkg/services/team/teamapi/api.go` - Main TeamAPI that registers team routes\n   - `/workspace/pkg/services/team/teamapi/team.go` - Team endpoint handlers\n\n5. **External Group Mapping API (K8s-style)**:\n   - `/workspace/pkg/registry/apis/iam/externalgroupmapping/models.go` - Defines the TeamGroupsHandler interface\n   - `/workspace/pkg/registry/apis/iam/externalgroupmapping/team_groups_noop.go` - Noop implementation for teams without enterprise\n\n6. **IAM API Registration**:\n   - `/workspace/pkg/registry/apis/iam/register.go` - Registers team group handlers with the API group\n   - `/workspace/pkg/registry/apis/iam/models.go` - API builder with teamGroupsHandler\n\n### Team Service:\n\n7. **Team Service Implementation**:\n   - `/workspace/pkg/services/team/teamimpl/team.go` - Core team service implementation\n   - `/workspace/pkg/services/team/teamimpl/store.go` - Team database store\n\n### LDAP Group Synchronization:\n\n8. **LDAP Groups Service**:\n   - `/workspace/pkg/services/ldap/ldap_groups.go` - LDAP groups service interface and OSS implementation\n   - `/workspace/pkg/services/ldap/ldap.go` - LDAP user and group extraction logic\n   - `/workspace/pkg/services/ldap/model.go` - LDAP configuration models for group mappings\n   - `/workspace/pkg/services/ldap/settings.go` - LDAP group settings and configuration\n\n9. **LDAP API Service**:\n   - `/workspace/pkg/services/ldap/api/service.go` - LDAP authentication service with team sync support\n\n10. **LDAP Authentication Client**:\n    - `/workspace/pkg/services/authn/clients/ldap.go` - LDAP authentication client with team sync parameters\n\n### Type Definitions:\n\n11. **API Specs** (Generated):\n    - `/workspace/public/api-enterprise-spec.json` - Enterprise API specification with team groups endpoints\n    - `/workspace/public/api-merged.json` - Merged API specification\n    - `/workspace/packages/grafana-openapi/src/api/openapi3.json` - OpenAPI 3 specification\n\n12. **Feature Flags**:\n    - `/workspace/pkg/services/featuremgmt/toggles_gen.go` - Feature flags for team sync (`teamsync`) and K8s external group mappings API\n    - `/workspace/pkg/services/featuremgmt/registry.go` - Feature toggle registry\n\n### Documentation:\n\n13. **API Documentation**:\n    - `/workspace/docs/sources/developer-resources/api-reference/http-api/team_sync.md` - Team Sync API reference\n\n14. **Setup/Configuration Docs**:\n    - `/workspace/docs/sources/setup-grafana/configure-access/configure-team-sync.md` - Team sync configuration guide\n    - `/workspace/docs/sources/setup-grafana/configure-access/configure-authentication/saml/configure-saml-team-role-mapping/_index.md` - SAML team role mapping\n\n### Test Utilities:\n\n15. **Test Handlers**:\n    - `/workspace/packages/grafana-test-utils/src/handlers/api/teams/handlers.ts` - Mock handlers for team groups API in tests\n\n### Key API Endpoints Implemented:\n- `GET /api/teams/:teamId/groups` - Get external groups for a team\n- `POST /api/teams/:teamId/groups` - Add external group to team\n- `DELETE /api/teams/:teamId/groups` - Remove external group from team\n- `GET /api/teams/:teamId/groups/search` - Search team groups\n\nThese files collectively implement the complete team group synchronization feature, including LDAP group sync, OAuth/SAML group mapping, and the REST APIs for managing external group mappings to Grafana teams.",
      "type": "text"
    }

Insert the value of the group you want to sync with. This becomes the Grafana `GroupID`.\\n54:    Examples:\\n55:    - For LDAP, this is the LDAP distinguished name (DN) of LDAP group you want to synchronize with the team.\\n56:    - For Auth Proxy, this is the value we receive as part of the custom `Groups` header.\\n57: \\n58: \",\"endLine\":58,\"startLine\":42},{\"content\":\"2: aliases:\\n3:   - ../setup-grafana/configure-security/configure-team-sync/ # /docs/grafana/next/setup-grafana/setup-grafana/configure-security/configure-team-sync/\\n4:   - ../../auth/team-sync/ # /docs/grafana/next/auth/team-sync/\\n5:   - ../../enterprise/team-sync/ # /docs/grafana/next/enterprise/team-sync/\\n6:   - ../configure-security/configure-team-sync/ # /docs/grafana/next/setup-grafana/configure-security/configure-team-sync/\\n7: description: Learn how to use Team Sync to synchronize between your authentication provider teams and Grafana teams.\\n8: labels:\\n9: \",\"endLine\":9,\"startLine\":2}],\"file\":\"docs/sources/setup-grafana/configure-access/configure-team-sync.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/grafana/grafana\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/grafana/grafana/-/blob/docs/sources/setup-grafana/configure-access/configure-team-sync.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"62: \\n63:   const teamGroupSync: NavModelItem = {\\n64:     active: false,\\n65:     icon: 'sync',\\n66:     id: `team-groupsync-${team.uid}`,\\n67:     text: t('teams.build-nav-model.team-group-sync.text.external-group-sync', 'External group sync'),\\n68:     url: `org/teams/edit/${team.uid}/groupsync`,\\n69:   };\\n70: \",\"endLine\":70,\"startLine\":62},{\"content\":\"86:     navModel.children!.push({\\n87:       ...teamGroupSync,\\n88:       tabSuffix: () => ProBadge({ experimentId: isLoadingTeam ? \\n233: ### SCIM vs Team Sync\\n234: \\n235: \",\"endLine\":235,\"startLine\":232},{\"content\":\"249: \\n250: SCIM Group Sync provides several advantages over Team Sync:\\n251: \\n252: - **Automatic team creation:** SCIM automatically creates Grafana teams when new groups are added to the identity provider\\n253: - **Automatic team deletion:** SCIM removes teams when their corresponding groups are deleted from the identity provider\\n254: - **Real-time updates:** Team memberships are updated immediately when group assignments change\\n255: - **Simplified management:** No need to manually create teams in Grafana before mapping them\\n256: \\n257: ### How team synchronization works\\n258: \\n259: SCIM manages teams through the following process:\\n260: \\n261: \",\"endLine\":261,\"startLine\":249},{\"content\":\"215: \\n216: ## Team provisioning with SCIM\\n217: \\n218: SCIM provides automated team management capabilities that go beyond what Team Sync offers. While Team Sync only maps identity provider groups to existing Grafana teams, SCIM can automatically create and delete teams based on group changes in the identity p ... [line truncated]\\n219: \\n220: \",\"endLine\":220,\"startLine\":215}],\"file\":\"docs/sources/setup-grafana/configure-access/configure-scim-provisioning/manage-users-teams/_index.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/grafana/grafana\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/grafana/grafana/-/blob/docs/sources/setup-grafana/configure-access/configure-scim-provisioning/manage-users-teams/_index.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"238: \\n239: ### Team Sync\\n240: \\n241: \",\"endLine\":241,\"startLine\":238},{\"content\":\"318: \\n319: [Learn more about Team Sync](../../configure-team-sync/)\\n320: \\n321: \",\"endLine\":321,\"startLine\":318},{\"content\":\"254: \\n255: You use the `X-WEBAUTH-GROUPS` header to send the team information for each user. Specifically, the set of Grafana's group IDs that the user belongs to.\\n256: \\n257: First, we need to set up the mapping between your authentication provider and Grafana. Follow [these instructions](../../configure-team-sync/#synchronize-a-grafana-team-with-an-external-group) to add groups to a team within Grafana.\\n258: \\n259: \",\"endLine\":259,\"startLine\":254}],\"file\":\"docs/sources/setup-grafana/configure-access/configure-authentication/auth-proxy/index.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/grafana/grafana\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/grafana/grafana/-/blob/docs/sources/setup-grafana/configure-access/configure-authentication/auth-proxy/index.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"37:   signoutRedirectUrl: '',\\n38:   skipOrgRoleSync: false,\\n39:   teamIds: [],\\n40:   teamIdsAttributePath: '',\\n41:   teamsUrl: '',\\n42:   tlsClientCa: '',\\n43: \",\"endLine\":43,\"startLine\":37},{\"content\":\"11:   allowedDomains: [],\\n12:   allowedGroups: [],\\n13:   allowedOrganizations: [],\\n14: \",\"endLine\":14,\"startLine\":11},{\"content\":\"29:   extra: {},\\n30:   groupsAttributePath: '',\\n31:   hostedDomain: '',\\n32: \",\"endLine\":32,\"startLine\":29}],\"file\":\"public/app/features/auth-config/utils/data.ts\",\"repo\":\"github.com/grafana/grafana\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/grafana/grafana/-/blob/public/app/features/auth-config/utils/data.ts\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"110: \\n111: ## Team sync\\n112: \\n113: \",\"endLine\":113,\"startLine\":110},{\"content\":\"116: \\n117: [Team Sync](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/<GRAFANA_VERSION>/setup-grafana/configure-access/configure-team-sync/) is a feature that allows you to map groups from your identity provider to Grafana teams. This is useful if you want to give your users acces ... [line truncated]\\n118: \\n119: To enable teamsync, you need to add a `groups` mapper to the client configuration in Keycloak.\\n120: This will add the `groups` claim to the id_token. You can then use the `groups` claim to map groups to teams in Grafana.\\n121: \\n122: \",\"endLine\":122,\"startLine\":116},{\"content\":\"98: {{< admonition type=\\\"warning\\\" >}}\\n99: These scopes do not add group claims to the `id_token`. Without group claims, teamsync will not work. Teamsync is covered further down in this document.\\n100: {{< /admonition >}}\\n101: \",\"endLine\":101,\"startLine\":98}],\"file\":\"docs/sources/setup-grafana/configure-access/configure-authentication/keycloak/index.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/grafana/grafana\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/grafana/grafana/-/blob/docs/sources/setup-grafana/configure-access/configure-authentication/keycloak/index.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"14: export const reducer = combineReducers({\\n15:   promRules: createAsyncMapSlice('promRules', fetchPromRulesAction, ({ rulesSourceName }) => rulesSourceName).reducer,\\n16:   rulerRules: createAsyncMapSlice('rulerRules', fetchRulerRulesAction, ({ rulesSourceName }) => rulesSourceName)\\n17:     .reducer,\\n18:   saveAMConfig: createAsyncSlice('saveAMConfig', updateAlertManagerConfigAction).reducer,\\n19:   deleteAMConfig: createAsyncSlice('deleteAMConfig', deleteAlertManagerConfigAction).reducer,\\n20:   amAlertGroups: createAsyncMapSlice(\\n21:     'amAlertGroups',\\n22:     fetchAlertGroupsAction,\\n23:     (alertManagerSourceName) => alertManagerSourceName\\n24: \",\"endLine\":24,\"startLine\":14},{\"content\":\"2: \\n3: import { createAsyncMapSlice, createAsyncSlice } from '../utils/redux';\\n4: \\n5: \",\"endLine\":5,\"startLine\":2},{\"content\":\"6:   deleteAlertManagerConfigAction,\\n7:   fetchAlertGroupsAction,\\n8:   fetchGrafanaAnnotationsAction,\\n9: \",\"endLine\":9,\"startLine\":6}],\"file\":\"public/app/features/alerting/unified/state/reducers.ts\",\"repo\":\"github.com/grafana/grafana\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/grafana/grafana/-/blob/public/app/features/alerting/unified/state/reducers.ts\"}]}"

In our case, the Loki team which has an ID of \\\"2\\\".\\n291: curl -H \\\"X-WEBAUTH-USER: admin\\\" -H \\\"X-WEBAUTH-GROUPS: lokiteamOnExternalSystem\\\" http://localhost:3000/api/teams/2/groups\\n292: [\\n293: \",\"endLine\":293,\"startLine\":289},{\"content\":\"261: ```bash\\n262: # First, inspect your teams and obtain the corresponding ID of the team we want to inspect the groups for.\\n263: curl -H \\\"X-WEBAUTH-USER: admin\\\" -H \\\"X-WEBAUTH-GROUPS: lokiteamOnExternalSystem\\\" http://localhost:3000/api/teams/search\\n264: {\\n265: \",\"endLine\":265,\"startLine\":261},{\"content\":\"254: \\n255: You use the `X-WEBAUTH-GROUPS` header to send the team information for each user. Specifically, the set of Grafana's group IDs that the user belongs to.\\n256: \\n257: First, we need to set up the mapping between your authentication provider and Grafana. Follow [these instructions](../../configure-team-sync/#synchronize-a-grafana-team-with-an-external-group) to add groups to a team within Grafana.\\n258: \\n259: \",\"endLine\":259,\"startLine\":254}],\"file\":\"docs/sources/setup-grafana/configure-access/configure-authentication/auth-proxy/index.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/grafana/grafana\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/grafana/grafana/-/blob/docs/sources/setup-grafana/configure-access/configure-authentication/auth-proxy/index.md\"}]}"

\\n430: \\t// Alert scopes are divided into two groups. The internal (to Grafana) and the external ones.\\n431: \\t// For the Grafana ones, given we have ACID control we're able to provide better granularity by defining CRUD options.\\n432: \\t// For the external ones, we only have read and write permissions due to the lack of atomicity control of the external system.\\n433: \\n434: \",\"endLine\":434,\"startLine\":429},{\"content\":\"412: \\n413: \\t// Team related actions\\n414: \\tActionTeamsCreate           = \\\"teams:create\\\"\\n415: \\tActionTeamsDelete           = \\\"teams:delete\\\"\\n416: \\tActionTeamsRead             = \\\"teams:read\\\"\\n417: \\tActionTeamsWrite            = \\\"teams:write\\\"\\n418: \\tActionTeamsPermissionsRead  = \\\"teams.permissions:read\\\"\\n419: \\tActionTeamsPermissionsWrite = \\\"teams.permissions:write\\\"\\n420: \\n421: \\t// Team related scopes\\n422: \\tScopeTeamsAll = \\\"teams:*\\\"\\n423: \\n424: \",\"endLine\":424,\"startLine\":412},{\"content\":\"235: \\tRoles        []string\\n236: \\tTeamIDs      []int64\\n237: \\tRolePrefixes []string\\n238: \",\"endLine\":238,\"startLine\":235}],\"file\":\"pkg/services/accesscontrol/models.go\",\"repo\":\"github.com/grafana/grafana\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/grafana/grafana/-/blob/pkg/services/accesscontrol/models.go\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"238: \\n239: ### Team Sync\\n240: \\n241: \",\"endLine\":241,\"startLine\":238},{\"content\":\"294:     \\\"orgId\\\": 1,\\n295:     \\\"teamId\\\": 2,\\n296:     \\\"groupId\\\": \\\"lokiTeamOnExternalSystem\\\"\\n297:   }\\n298: \",\"endLine\":298,\"startLine\":294},{\"content\":\"254: \\n255: You use the `X-WEBAUTH-GROUPS` header to send the team information for each user. Follow [these instructions](../../configure-team-sync/#synchronize-a-grafana-team-with-an-external-group) to add groups to a team within Grafana.\\n258: \\n259: \",\"endLine\":259,\"startLine\":254}],\"file\":\"docs/sources/setup-grafana/configure-access/configure-authentication/auth-proxy/index.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/grafana/grafana\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/grafana/grafana/-/blob/docs/sources/setup-grafana/configure-access/configure-authentication/auth-proxy/index.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"66: \\n67: The following `External Group ID`s would be valid for input in the desired team's _External group sync_ tab:\\n68: \\n69: - `admins_group`\\n70: - `division_1`\\n71: \",\"endLine\":71,\"startLine\":66},{\"content\":\"15: \\n16: # Configure team sync for SAML\\n17: \\n18: \",\"endLine\":18,\"startLine\":15},{\"content\":\"21: \\n22: To use SAML Team sync, set [`assertion_attribute_groups`](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/<GRAFANA_VERSION>/setup-grafana/configure-grafana/enterprise-configuration/#assertion_attribute_groups) to the attribute name where you store user groups. [line truncated]\\n1939: \",\"endLine\":1939,\"startLine\":1932},{\"content\":\"2118: };\\n2119: export type GetGroupsExternalGroupMapping = {\\n2120:   externalGroup: string;\\n2121:   name: string;\\n2122: \",\"endLine\":2122,\"startLine\":2118}],\"file\":\"packages/grafana-api-clients/src/clients/rtkq/iam/v0alpha1/endpoints.gen.ts\",\"repo\":\"github.com/grafana/grafana\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/grafana/grafana/-/blob/packages/grafana-api-clients/src/clients/rtkq/iam/v0alpha1/endpoints.gen.ts\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"133: func (fam *RemoteSecondaryForkedAlertmanager) ApplyConfig(ctx context.Context, config *models.AlertConfiguration) error {\\n134: \\tvar wg sync.WaitGroup\\n135: \\twg.Add(1)\\n136: \\t// Figure out if we need to sync the external Alertmanager in another goroutine.\\n137: \\tgo func() {\\n138: \",\"endLine\":138,\"startLine\":133},{\"content\":\"85: \\t\\t\\tl := log.New(\\\"ngalert.forked-alertmanager.remote-secondary\\\")\\n86: \\t\\t\\tremoteAM, err := NewAlertmanager(ctx, cfg, notifier.NewFileStore(cfg.OrgID, store), crypto, autogenFn, m, t, features)\\n87: \\t\\t\\tif err != nil && withRemoteState {\\n88: \",\"endLine\":88,\"startLine\":85},{\"content\":\"36: \\torgID int64\\n37: \\tstore configStore\\n38: \\n39: \",\"endLine\":39,\"startLine\":36}],\"file\":\"pkg/services/ngalert/remote/remote_secondary_forked_alertmanager.go\",\"repo\":\"github.com/grafana/grafana\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/grafana/grafana/-/blob/pkg/services/ngalert/remote/remote_secondary_forked_alertmanager.go\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"178: \\n179: ## User synchronization between Grafana and identity providers\\n180: \\n181: When connecting Grafana to an identity provider, it's important to think beyond just the initial authentication setup. You should also think about the maintenance of user bases and roles. Using Grafana's team and role synchronization features ensures that  ... [line truncated]\\n182: \\n183: ### Team sync\\n184: \\n185: Team sync is a feature that allows you to synchronize teams or groups from your authentication provider with teams in Grafana. This means that users of specific teams or groups in LDAP, OAuth, or SAML will be automatically added or removed as members of co ... [line truncated]\\n186: \\n187: \",\"endLine\":187,\"startLine\":178},{\"content\":\"191: {{< admonition type=\\\"note\\\" >}}\\n192: Team synchronization occurs only when a user logs in. However, if you are using LDAP, it is possible to enable active background synchronization. This allows for the continuous synchronization of teams.\\n193: {{< /admonition >}}\\n194: \",\"endLine\":194,\"startLine\":191},{\"content\":\"194: \\n195: ### Role Sync\\n196: \\n197: Grafana can synchronize basic roles from your authentication provider by mapping attributes from the identity provider to the user role in Grafana. This means that users with specific attributes, like role, team, or group membership in LDAP, OAuth, or SAML ... [line truncated]\\n198: \\n199: ### Organization sync\\n200: \\n201: Organization sync is the process of binding all the users from an organization in Grafana. This delegates the role of managing users to the identity provider. This way, there's no need to manage user access from Grafana because the identity provider will b ... [line truncated]\\n202: \\n203: With organization sync, you can assign users from identity provider groups to corresponding Grafana organizations. This functionality is similar to role sync but with the added benefit of specifying the organization that a user belongs to for a particular  ... [line truncated]\\n204: \\n205: \",\"endLine\":205,\"startLine\":194}],\"file\":\"docs/sources/setup-grafana/configure-access/_index.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/grafana/grafana\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/grafana/grafana/-/blob/docs/sources/setup-grafana/configure-access/_index.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"232: \\n233: ### SCIM vs Team Sync\\n234: \\n235: \",\"endLine\":235,\"startLine\":232},{\"content\":\"238: \\n239: Choose one team synchronization method:\\n240: \\n241: - If you enable SCIM Group Sync, disable Team Sync and use SCIM for team management\\n242: - If you prefer Team Sync, do not enable SCIM Group Sync\\n243: \\n244: \",\"endLine\":244,\"startLine\":238},{\"content\":\"249: \\n250: SCIM Group Sync provides several advantages over Team Sync:\\n251: \\n252: - **Automatic team creation:** SCIM automatically creates Grafana teams when new groups are added to the identity provider\\n253: - **Automatic team deletion:** SCIM removes teams when their corresponding groups are deleted from the identity provider\\n254: - **Real-time updates:** Team memberships are updated immediately when group assignments change\\n255: - **Simplified management:** No need to manually create teams in Grafana before mapping them\\n256: \\n257: ### How team synchronization works\\n258: \\n259: SCIM manages teams through the following process:\\n260: \\n261: Group assignment:\\n262: \\n263: - User is assigned to groups in the identity provider\\n264: - SCIM detects group membership changes\\n265: \\n266: Team creation and mapping:\\n267: \\n268: - Creates Grafana teams for new identity provider groups\\n269: - Maps users to appropriate teams\\n270: - Removes users from teams when group membership changes\\n271: \\n272: Team membership maintenance:\\n273: \\n274: ... [chunk truncated]\",\"endLine\":278,\"startLine\":249}],\"file\":\"docs/sources/setup-grafana/configure-access/configure-scim-provisioning/manage-users-teams/_index.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/grafana/grafana\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/grafana/grafana/-/blob/docs/sources/setup-grafana/configure-access/configure-scim-provisioning/manage-users-teams/_index.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"113: - **403** - Permission denied\\n114: - **404** - Team not found\\n115: \\n116: ## Remove External Group\\n117: \\n118: `DELETE /api/teams/:teamId/groups?groupId=external-group-id`\\n119: \\n120: \",\"endLine\":120,\"startLine\":113},{\"content\":\"2: aliases:\\n3:   - ../../../http_api/external_group_sync/ # /docs/grafana/next/http_api/external_group_sync/\\n4:   - ../../../developers/http_api/external_group_sync/ # /docs/grafana/next/developers/http_api/external_group_sync/\\n5:   - ../../../developers/http_api/team_sync/ # /docs/grafana/next/developers/http_api/team_sync/\\n6: canonical: https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/developer-resources/api-reference/http-api/team_sync/\\n7: description: Grafana Team Sync HTTP API\\n8: keywords:\\n9: \",\"endLine\":9,\"startLine\":2},{\"content\":\"25: \\n26: # Team Sync API\\n27: \\n28: > Team Sync is only available in Grafana Enterprise. Read more about [Grafana Enterprise](/docs/grafana/latest/introduction/grafana-enterprise/).\\n29: \\n30: \",\"endLine\":30,\"startLine\":25}],\"file\":\"docs/sources/developer-resources/api-reference/http-api/team_sync.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/grafana/grafana\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/grafana/grafana/-/blob/docs/sources/developer-resources/api-reference/http-api/team_sync.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"91: \\tuserSearchHandler                 *user.SearchHandler\\n92: \\tteamSearch                        *TeamSearchHandler\\n93: \\texternalGroupMappingSearchHandler externalgroupmapping.SearchHandler\\n94: \\n95: \\tteamGroupsHandler externalgroupmapping.TeamGroupsHandler\\n96: \\n97: \",\"endLine\":97,\"startLine\":91},{\"content\":\"50: type IdentityAccessManagementAPIBuilder struct {\\n51: \\t// Stores\\n52: \\tstore legacy.LegacyIdentityStore\\n53: \\n54: \\tuserLegacyStore                  *user.LegacyStore\\n55: \\tsaLegacyStore                    *serviceaccount.LegacyStore\\n56: \\tlegacyTeamStore                  *team.LegacyStore\\n57: \\tteamBindingLegacyStore           *teambinding.LegacyBindingStore\\n58: \\tssoLegacyStore                   *sso.LegacyStore\\n59: \\tcoreRolesStorage                 CoreRoleStorageBackend\\n60: \",\"endLine\":60,\"startLine\":50},{\"content\":\"61: \\tglobalRoleApiInstaller           GlobalRoleApiInstaller\\n62: \\tteamLBACApiInstaller             TeamLBACApiInstaller\\n63: \\texternalGroupMappingApiInstaller ExternalGroupMappingApiInstaller\\n64: \\tresourcePermissionsStorage       resource.StorageBackend\\n65: \",\"endLine\":65,\"startLine\":61}],\"file\":\"pkg/registry/apis/iam/models.go\",\"repo\":\"github.com/grafana/grafana\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/grafana/grafana/-/blob/pkg/registry/apis/iam/models.go\"}]}"

\\n179: ## User synchronization between Grafana and identity providers\\n180: \\n181: When connecting Grafana to an identity provider, it's important to think beyond just the initial authentication setup. You should also think about the maintenance of user bases and roles. Using Grafana's team and role synchronization features ensures that  ... [line truncated]\\n182: \\n183: ### Team sync\\n184: \\n185: Team sync is a feature that allows you to synchronize teams or groups from your authentication provider with teams in Grafana. This means that users of specific teams or groups in LDAP, OAuth, or SAML will be automatically added or removed as members of co ... [line truncated]\\n186: \\n187: \",\"endLine\":187,\"startLine\":178},{\"content\":\"142: \\n143: You can assign roles through the user interface or APIs, establish them through Terraform, or synchronize them automatically via an external IAM provider.\\n144: \\n145: \",\"endLine\":145,\"startLine\":142},{\"content\":\"191: {{< admonition type=\\\"note\\\" >}}\\n192: Team synchronization occurs only when a user logs in. However, if you are using LDAP, it is possible to enable active background synchronization. This allows for the continuous synchronization of teams.\\n193: {{< /admonition >}}\\n194: \",\"endLine\":194,\"startLine\":191}],\"file\":\"docs/sources/setup-grafana/configure-access/_index.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/grafana/grafana\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/grafana/grafana/-/blob/docs/sources/setup-grafana/configure-access/_index.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"97: SCIM group sync (`group_sync_enabled = true`) and Team Sync cannot be enabled simultaneously\\n106: - You can use SCIM user sync (`user_sync_enabled = true`) alongside Team Sync\\n107: - For more details about migration and compatibility, see [SCIM vs Team Sync](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/<GRAFANA_VERSION>/setup-grafana/configure-access/configure-scim-provisioning/manage-users-teams/#scim-vs-team-sync)\\n108:   {{< /admonition >}}\\n109: \",\"endLine\":109,\"startLine\":102},{\"content\":\"138: | ------------------------------ | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------- |\\n139: | `user_sync_enabled`            | Yes      | Enable SCIM user provisioning. When enabled, Grafana will create, update, and deactivate users based on SCIM requests from your identity provider.                                         | `false` |\\n140: | `group_sync_enabled`           | No       | Enable SCIM group provisioning. When enabled, Grafana will create, update, and delete teams based on SCIM requests from your identity provider. Cannot be enabled if Team Sync is enabled. | `false` |\\n141: | `reject_non_provisioned_users` | No       | When enabled, prevents non-SCIM provisioned users from signing in. Cloud Portal users can always sign in regardless of this setting.                                                       | `false` |\\n142: \",\"endLine\":142,\"startLine\":138},{\"content\":\"78: | ------------------------------ | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------- |\\n79: | `Enable Group Sync`            | No       | Enable SCIM group provisioning. When enabled, Grafana will create, update, and delete teams based on SCIM requests from your identity provider. Cannot be enabled if Team Sync is enabled. | `false` |\\n80: | `Reject Non-Provisioned Users` | No       | When enabled, prevents non-SCIM provisioned users from signing in. Cloud Portal users can always sign in regardless of this setting.                                                       | `false` |\\n81: | `Enable User Sync`             | Yes      | Enable SCIM user provisioning. When enabled, Grafana will create, update, and deactivate users based on SCIM requests from your identity provider.                                         | `false` |\\n82: \\n83: \",\"endLine\":83,\"startLine\":78}],\"file\":\"docs/sources/setup-grafana/configure-access/configure-scim-provisioning/_index.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/grafana/grafana\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/grafana/grafana/-/blob/docs/sources/setup-grafana/configure-access/configure-scim-provisioning/_index.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"246: \\n247: {{< figure src=\\\"/static/img/docs/enterprise/oauth-synced-user-9-1.png\\\" max-width=\\\"750px\\\" caption=\\\"Non-interactive view of a user synced via OAuth\\\" >}}\\n248: \\n249: #### Support wildcards for LDAP groups in team sync\\n250: \\n251: Team sync lets you set up synchronization between your authentication provider's teams and Grafana teams.\\n252: To leverage your existing Active Directory in an efficient way without having to create multiple teams, you can now use wildcards when configuring LDAP groups, so that multiple Active Directory groups can be added at once to a single team.\\n253: \\n254: {{< figure src=\\\"/static/img/docs/enterprise/ldap-wildcard-teamsync-9-1.png\\\" max-width=\\\"750px\\\" caption=\\\"Using wildcard mapping for team sync\\\" >}}\\n255: \\n256: \",\"endLine\":256,\"startLine\":246},{\"content\":\"41: Service accounts have been in beta since Grafana v8.5.\\n42: During that time, we've improved the UI and migration path from API keys, made it possible to add service accounts to teams, and inherit team permissions.\\n43: To learn more about service accounts, see the [documentation](../../administration/service-accounts/).\\n44: \",\"endLine\":44,\"startLine\":41},{\"content\":\"120: \\n121: #### (Beta) APM table\\n122: \\n123: \",\"endLine\":85,\"startLine\":78}],\"file\":\"public/app/features/teams/state/navModel.ts\",\"repo\":\"github.com/grafana/grafana\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/grafana/grafana/-/blob/public/app/features/teams/state/navModel.ts\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"170: \\n171: You can now leave a team empty without any users, or have only Members in a team.\\n172: This helps you sync teams and users from a single sign-on provider like Active Directory or Okta, or if you use teams as collections of permissions.\\n173: Previously, teams required at least one Admin user to be in a team.\\n174: \\n175: {{< figure src=\\\"/static/img/docs/manage-users/member-only-team-9-2.png\\\" max-width=\\\"750px\\\" caption=\\\"Creating a team without an Admin user\\\" >}}\\n176: \\n177: \",\"endLine\":177,\"startLine\":170},{\"content\":\"209: \\n210: ### Skip organization role sync\\n211: \\n212: \",\"endLine\":212,\"startLine\":209},{\"content\":\"213: \\n214: If you use a SAML identity provider to manage your users but prefer to assign roles and permissions in the Grafana UI or via API, you can now enable a configuration option to skip user organization and roles synchronization with your SAML provider.\\n215: \\n216: Use the `skip_org_role_sync` configuration option when configuring SAML to prevent synchronization with SAML roles and make user roles editable from within Grafana.\\n217: \\n218: \",\"endLine\":218,\"startLine\":213}],\"file\":\"docs/sources/whatsnew/whats-new-in-v9-2.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/grafana/grafana\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/grafana/grafana/-/blob/docs/sources/whatsnew/whats-new-in-v9-2.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"105: - `GET /api/access-control/datasources/:uid` for listing data source permissions\\n839: - `POST /api/access-control/datasources/:uid/users/:id`, `POST /api/access-control/datasources/:uid/teams/:id` and `POST /api/access-control/datasources/:uid/buildInRoles/:id` for adding or removing data source permissions\\n840: \\n841: If you are using Terraform Grafana provider to manage data source permissions, you will need to upgrade your provider to [version 2.6.0](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/grafana/grafana/2.6.0/docs) or newer to ensure that data source permission prov ... [line truncated]\\n842: \\n843: \",\"endLine\":843,\"startLine\":838},{\"content\":\"1603: - **Mysql Tests:** Mysql5.7 integration tests, use utf8mb4 charset. (Enterprise)\\n1604: - **RBAC:** Validate provided Action for Create/Update Role. (Enterprise)\\n1605: - **Reports:** Activate draft reports. (Enterprise)\\n1606: \",\"endLine\":1606,\"startLine\":1603},{\"content\":\"1577: - **Theme:** Change dark theme borders to improve contrast on primary background. [#67699](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/67699), [@torkelo](https://github.com/torkelo)\\n1578: - **Alerting:** Refactor the ImageStore/Provider to provide image URL/bytes. [#67693](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/67693), [@santihernandezc](https://github.com/santihernandezc)\\n1579: - **Alerting:** Allow to tab onto elements for a11y. [#67684](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/67684), [@VikaCep](https://github.com/VikaCep)\\n1580: \",\"endLine\":1580,\"startLine\":1577}],\"file\":\".changelog-archive/CHANGELOG.10.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/grafana/grafana\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/grafana/grafana/-/blob/.changelog-archive/CHANGELOG.10.md\"}]}"

Enter the credentials accordingly.\\n293: \",\"endLine\":293,\"startLine\":290},{\"content\":\"399: \\n400: We've added support for the Microsoft Teams contact points when using an external Alertmanager.\\n401: \\n402: \",\"endLine\":402,\"startLine\":399},{\"content\":\"273: \\n274: In the Datadog data source, you can now block API requests for metric queries based on upstream rate limits. **Dashboards:** Remove deprecated dashboard id endpoints [#117227](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/117227), [@stephaniehingtgen](https://github.com/stephaniehingtgen)\\n132: - **DashboardsAPI:** Deprecate /api/dashboards/home [#115333](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/115333), [@ryantxu](https://github.com/ryantxu)\\n133: - **DataSources:** Deprecate api routes with name and internal IDs [#116391](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/116391), [@ryantxu](https://github.com/ryantxu)\\n134: - **DataSources:** Update SDKs in support of auth service [#112101](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/112101), [@njvrzm](https://github.com/njvrzm)\\n135: \",\"endLine\":135,\"startLine\":131},{\"content\":\"3994: - **Auditlog:** Refactor action to post-action in default auditlogging. (Enterprise)\\n3995: - **Plugins:** Make grafana-com API URL usage consistent. (Enterprise)\\n3996: - **Plugins:** Make grafana-com API URL usage consistent. (Enterprise)\\n3997: - **Caching:** Implement mtls-enabled memcached integration. (Enterprise)\\n3998: - **OpenAPI:** Document the datasource caching API. (Enterprise)\\n3999: \\n4000: \",\"endLine\":4000,\"startLine\":3994},{\"content\":\"4349: - **InfluxDB:** Fix alias interpolation when it has $\\\\_\\\\_interval or multiple tags. [#84940](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/84940), [@itsmylife](https://github.com/itsmylife)\\n4350: - **Alerting:** Stop returning autogen routes for non-admin on api/v2/status. [#84864](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/84864), [@JacobsonMT](https://github.com/JacobsonMT)\\n4351: - **Alerting:** Fix broken panelId links. [#84839](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/84839), [@gillesdemey](https://github.com/gillesdemey)\\n4352: \",\"endLine\":4352,\"startLine\":4349}],\"file\":\"CHANGELOG.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/grafana/grafana\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/grafana/grafana/-/blob/CHANGELOG.md\"}]}"
